COURSE WORKBOOK
with Gretta Van Riel, Rob Ward, Chase Dimond
Richard Li, Nick Shackelford
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

-- 4 of 7 --

In this lesson, I’m going to show you how to look at your account and
structure it up for scale, as well as walk you through my Success Metrics
Spreadsheet and how to use it.
This resource will allow you to quickly make decisions in your ad account on
which creative to keep or kill, in only 15-25 minutes a day, so that you can
stay focused on the business and coming up with new angles.
Back in the day, when you wanted to scale a physical brick-and-mortar
store, what did you have to do? You had to open up multiple physical
locations, hire and train staff, etc. Today, in order to scale our ONLINE stores,
we’re simply adding dollars to a budget.
What does this mean for your account? You need to set it up for scaling in
a way that’s structured properly, and have an understanding of the different
views inside your FB account, so you can quickly make decisions along the
way.
Throughout this video demo, I walk you through the ins and outs of your
FB Ads account, and my Success Metrics Spreadsheet which you can
download below and start using right away to make fast decisions to scale
your spend.
Let’s also look at the funnel:
LESSON:
FOUNDATIONS (PART A):

For most:
• 	Facebook is a full funnel tool.
Allowing brands to:
• 	Create Demand.
• 	Nurture Demand.
• 	Capture Demand.
For some:
• 	Facebook is a Prospecting or top-of-funnel (TOF) tool.
Allowing brands to:
• 	Create Demand.
• 	Nurture Demand.
